tagline: An occupied nation becomes a control-war between imperial hierarchy, masked insurgency, and a will-hacking anomaly that turns strategy into governance engineering.
visualizationReason: Code Geass is fundamentally about leverage webs: Geass contracts, military command chains, political legitimacy, and betrayals all rewire who can issue orders to whom, so a node graph best exposes shifting control dependencies.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: The only ones who should kill are those prepared to be killed.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [COMMAND_OVERRIDE]: CONSENT LAYER BYPASSED — AUTHORITY GRAPH MUTATING IN REAL TIME

powerSystem.name: Geass
powerSystem.subtitle: Contract-Bound Command Exploit
powerSystem.loreDesc: A supernatural power granted through contracts with Code bearers. Each user manifests a personalized ability, and Lelouch's Geass can force absolute obedience once eye contact is established.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A per-user authority override protocol that writes temporary or permanent instructions directly into a target's decision layer. It is high impact but bound by strict trigger conditions and runaway-evolution risk.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Lelouch accidentally commands Euphemia to kill the Japanese, proving one unstable instruction can cascade into national-scale collapse.

powerSystem.name: Knightmare Frame Warfare
powerSystem.subtitle: Sakuradite-Powered Mobility Supremacy
powerSystem.loreDesc: Humanoid war machines define the military meta. Superior frames like the Lancelot and Guren repeatedly overturn larger conventional forces through speed and specialized systems.
powerSystem.systemDesc: An arms-race platform where sakuradite energy density, maneuver tech, and pilot cognition decide battlefield throughput. Technical edge compounds political edge by rapidly changing territorial control.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Suzaku's Lancelot breaks the Battle of Narita's momentum by outclassing legacy units and restoring Britannian operational control.

powerSystem.name: Symbols, Masks, and Broadcast Legitimacy
powerSystem.subtitle: Narrative Command Infrastructure
powerSystem.loreDesc: Zero's mask turns one exile prince into a global revolutionary symbol. Public declarations, trials, and spectacle warfare recruit civilians and destabilize imperial narrative control.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A legitimacy engine that converts perception into manpower, diplomatic recognition, and compliance. Information theater functions as strategic middleware between tactical victories and regime durability.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: The Black Rebellion proves Zero can reframe insurgency from terrorism into governance alternative, forcing global actors to re-evaluate allegiance.

characters.name: Lelouch Lamperouge
characters.title: Zero / Disruption Architect
characters.loreBio: An exiled Britannian prince who weaponizes strategy, theater, and Geass to shatter imperial rule and eventually script his own death as a political reset.
characters.systemBio: Primary mutation node: combines command override, information dominance, and sacrificial endgame planning to rewrite global authority routing.
characters.signatureMoment: Executes Zero Requiem, concentrating global hatred onto himself so his assassination can release a stability rebound.

characters.name: Suzaku Kururugi
characters.title: Knight of Zero / Enforcement Blade
characters.loreBio: The son of Japan's last prime minister who tries to reform Britannia from within, then becomes Lelouch's execution vector in Zero Requiem.
characters.systemBio: High-trust enforcement process with elite combat throughput; bridges institutional legitimacy and insurgent strategy at decisive transition points.
characters.signatureMoment: Accepts the permanent Zero role after killing Lelouch, turning personal guilt into long-term deterrent governance.

characters.name: C.C.
characters.title: Code Bearer / Contract Broker
characters.loreBio: An immortal who grants Geass and carries centuries of memory, steering Lelouch while seeking release from endless continuity.
characters.systemBio: Metaphysical gateway node that issues exploit permissions to selected actors and preserves hidden protocol knowledge across eras.
characters.signatureMoment: Restores Lelouch's strategic focus after the Black Knights fracture, keeping the final system rewrite executable.

characters.name: Stadtfeld, Kallen
characters.title: Black Knights Strike Ace
characters.loreBio: A frontline rebel whose dual identity mirrors occupied Japan's fracture; her loyalty to Zero sustains the movement through repeated crises.
characters.systemBio: Primary tactical damage dealer for insurgent operations; converts strategy into battlefield wins that keep the rebellion politically viable.
characters.signatureMoment: Defeats elite Britannian pilots in the Guren during the final campaign, preserving Black Knights combat parity.

characters.name: Charles zi Britannia
characters.title: Emperor / Ragnarök Architect
characters.loreBio: Britannia's emperor, who preaches ruthless hierarchy while secretly planning to erase individual lies and pain through collective consciousness merger.
characters.systemBio: Legacy root-admin node pursuing metaphysical totalization: replace plural human agency with a single static state in C's World.
characters.signatureMoment: Initiates Ragnarök Connection in the Thought Elevator, attempting a hard overwrite of human individuality.

characters.name: Schneizel el Britannia
characters.title: Prime Strategist / Damocles Controller
characters.loreBio: A calm imperial strategist who pursues peace through absolute fear, seizing Damocles and F.L.E.I.J.A. to freeze the world under permanent threat.
characters.systemBio: Stasis-optimization node: replaces political negotiation with omnipresent annihilation leverage, maximizing compliance through terror equilibrium.
characters.signatureMoment: Broadcasts Damocles as a global sword, demanding obedience under real-time F.L.E.I.J.A. deterrence.

characters.name: Nunnally Lamperouge
characters.title: Moral Anchor / Political Heir
characters.loreBio: Lelouch's sister and emotional core, repeatedly used as a legitimacy symbol by competing powers while refusing pure cynicism.
characters.systemBio: High-value legitimacy token whose consent state affects both rebel morale and imperial narrative control.
characters.signatureMoment: Confronts Lelouch's tyranny live, forcing the final plan to expose its ethical cost instead of hiding behind pure strategy.

characters.name: Jeremiah Gottwald
characters.title: The Orange / Loyal Counterweight
characters.loreBio: A disgraced Britannian officer rebuilt into a cybernetic fighter who ultimately becomes one of Lelouch's most reliable defenders.
characters.systemBio: Anti-Geass support node with cancel capability and high durability, critical for defending command architecture against hostile overrides.
characters.signatureMoment: Deploys Geass Canceller to neutralize memory control and restore strategic clarity during late-stage conflicts.

characters.name: Rolo Lamperouge
characters.title: Assassin Proxy
characters.loreBio: A manufactured brother figure assigned to monitor Lelouch, whose desperate need for belonging turns him from handler into sacrificial ally.
characters.systemBio: Temporal-perception jammer node used for close-range elimination and containment; unstable loyalty profile creates high volatility.
characters.signatureMoment: Burns out his own heart to save Lelouch, converting a surveillance asset into a terminal loyalty action.

relationships.loreDesc: Childhood friends become ideological opposites who still need each other to execute the final peace design.
relationships.systemDesc: Mutual dependency between disruption and legitimacy nodes; their convergence enables Zero Requiem's handoff protocol.

relationships.loreDesc: C.C. grants Lelouch Geass and anchors him when his identity fractures.
relationships.systemDesc: Core exploit user relies on gateway provider for permission access and hidden backend intelligence.

relationships.loreDesc: Kallen repeatedly stakes her life on Zero's campaigns despite secrecy and betrayals.
relationships.systemDesc: Primary tactical executor tied to central strategist; battlefield throughput sustains command credibility.

relationships.loreDesc: His promise to build a gentler world for Nunnally drives both rebellion and self-destruction.
relationships.systemDesc: Emotional objective variable that biases decision policy and can override optimal military calculus.

relationships.loreDesc: Son and father collide over whether pain should be erased through forced unity.
relationships.systemDesc: Competing root-governance models: plural agency with conflict vs single-state metaphysical overwrite.

relationships.loreDesc: Two master strategists clash over control of the endgame framework.
relationships.systemDesc: Optimization conflict between sacrificial regime reset and permanent terror-deterrence stasis.

relationships.loreDesc: Schneizel positions Nunnally as a humane face for Damocles authority.
relationships.systemDesc: Uses high-trust civilian node to increase acceptance of coercive deterrence architecture.

relationships.loreDesc: Rolo begins as Lelouch's surveillant and repeatedly sabotages his network before later sacrifice.
relationships.systemDesc: Embedded handler process compromises central node integrity before switching alignment under emotional load.

relationships.loreDesc: Jeremiah's absolutist loyalty gives Lelouch a rare dependable shield in the final phase.
relationships.systemDesc: Security-hardening node supplies anti-Geass defense and command continuity under betrayal pressure.

relationships.loreDesc: Suzaku eventually rejects imperial reform fantasies and opposes the regime's hidden agenda.
relationships.systemDesc: Internal enforcement asset exits incumbent hierarchy, reducing empire's legitimacy and response speed.

relationships.loreDesc: Suzaku's direct-action combat role counters Schneizel's strategic distance and deterrence posture.
relationships.systemDesc: High-mobility strike node disrupts static command platforms like Damocles when timing windows open.

relationships.loreDesc: C.C. opposes Charles's world-merger project despite sharing deep ties to Geass history.
relationships.systemDesc: Legacy protocol holder blocks monopolization of metaphysical backend by a single sovereign actor.

factions.name: Holy Britannian Empire
factions.loreDesc: Expansionist superpower enforcing nobility hierarchy and Number-system dehumanization across conquered areas.
factions.systemDesc: Centralized coercive state optimized for extraction, rank-based privilege, and military occupation throughput.

factions.name: Order of the Black Knights
factions.loreDesc: Zero-led resistance evolving from Japanese insurgency into transnational anti-imperial force.
factions.systemDesc: Asymmetric coalition that scales via symbolic legitimacy, selective terror, and federated military integration.

factions.name: Geass Order
factions.loreDesc: Secret institution researching and weaponizing Geass users for elite agendas.
factions.systemDesc: Metaphysical R&D layer producing controlled exploit users and memory-management operations.

factions.name: United Federation of Nations
factions.loreDesc: Post-colonial alliance framework attempting collective governance after imperial fracture.
factions.systemDesc: Distributed political protocol designed to prevent single-node hegemony through shared authority contracts.

rules.law: Geass Requires a Trigger and Evolves
rules.desc: Each Geass has conditions and can become uncontrollable as it matures.
rules.loreSubtitle: Power granted always carries a cost.
rules.systemSubtitle: Exploit capability expands faster than operator safety controls.

rules.desc: Britannian policy treats hierarchy and conquest as natural law.
rules.loreSubtitle: Compassion is coded as weakness in the imperial machine.
rules.systemSubtitle: Institutional incentives produce persistent oppression and rebellion feedback.

rules.desc: A movement survives only if people believe it can govern, not just fight.
rules.loreSubtitle: Zero's symbol is as important as any Knightmare.
rules.systemSubtitle: Narrative control functions as force multiplier for recruitment and compliance.

rules.law: Peace by Total Control Collapses Humanity
rules.desc: Both Ragnarök and Damocles promise stability by deleting freedom.
rules.loreSubtitle: A world without conflict risks becoming a world without people.
rules.systemSubtitle: Hard-stasis architectures remove adaptive agency and trigger catastrophic resistance.

anomalies.title: Euphemia Command Catastrophe
anomalies.loreDesc: A single accidental Geass command transforms a peace initiative into massacre.
anomalies.systemDesc: Operator-side failure converts tactical exploit into civilization-scale trust collapse.
anomalies.impact: Drives irreversible escalation and legitimizes total war narratives.

anomalies.title: Ragnarök Connection Attempt
anomalies.loreDesc: Charles and Marianne try to merge all consciousness in C's World.
anomalies.systemDesc: Metaphysical forced-unification protocol seeks to eliminate individual-state variance.
anomalies.impact: Creates existential conflict over whether suffering should be solved by deleting personhood.

anomalies.title: F.L.E.I.J.A. Warhead Deployment
anomalies.loreDesc: Localized superweapon use erases cities and shatters conventional deterrence logic.
anomalies.systemDesc: Instant mass-deletion event collapses prior escalation models and centralizes fear power.
anomalies.impact: Enables Damocles doctrine and compresses diplomacy into coercion under annihilation threat.

anomalies.title: Zero Requiem Execution
anomalies.loreDesc: Lelouch turns himself into global villain so his death can reset political hostility.
anomalies.systemDesc: Deliberate centralization of blame followed by symbolic decapitation to trigger multi-faction reconciliation.
anomalies.impact: Produces short-term stability through engineered catharsis rather than institutional perfection.

counterplay.title: Geass Overwrite vs Geass Canceller
counterplay.situation: Mind-control authority meets cancellation tech and memory restoration.
counterplay.resolution: Counter-nodes like Jeremiah reduce exploit lock-in and reopen strategic agency.

counterplay.title: Lancelot Mobility vs Guren Burst Damage
counterplay.situation: Precision speed duelist against high-output close-range breaker.
counterplay.resolution: Outcome depends on terrain, energy windows, and pilot prediction loops.

counterplay.title: Damocles Deterrence vs Distributed Rebellion
counterplay.situation: Single airborne terror platform against multi-theater resistance network.
counterplay.resolution: Rebels win only by degrading command center confidence and forcing exposure moments.

counterplay.title: Imperial Propaganda vs Zero Symbol
counterplay.situation: State narrative monopoly challenged by insurgent myth and media theater.
counterplay.resolution: When imperial violence is visible, Zero's legitimacy conversion rate spikes.

counterplay.title: Ragnarök Homogenization vs Individual Rejection
counterplay.situation: Collective consciousness merge confronted by demand to preserve separate wills.
counterplay.resolution: Lelouch's rejection preserves conflict-prone humanity over static unity.

causalEvents.event: Britannia conquers Japan and renames it Area 11
causalEvents.significance: Colonial occupation creates the grievance substrate for Zero's insurgency.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Japan's identity is stripped, and its people become 'Elevens' under systemic humiliation.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Identity erasure policy generates persistent resistance fuel and legitimacy vacuum.

causalEvents.significance: A powerless exile gains command-level exploit capability.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The contract births Zero and makes rebellion mathematically plausible.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Single-node capability upgrade enables asymmetric challenge to superior state hardware.

causalEvents.significance: Moderate political settlement becomes impossible after mass civilian slaughter.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The Special Administrative Zone dream dies in blood.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Trust network crash forces all actors toward maximalist strategies.

causalEvents.event: Lelouch seizes throne and launches Zero Requiem
causalEvents.significance: He centralizes global hatred to script a controlled post-assassination reset.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The tyrant role is assumed deliberately so the world can unite against one target.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Intentional villain bottleneck concentrates hostility, enabling coordinated release through symbolic elimination.

rankings.topTierLore: Actors who can alter nations or consciousness itself.
rankings.topTierSystem: Nodes with both strategic planning capacity and architecture-level override access.

tiers.name: Metaphysical Root Access
tiers.loreDesc: Code bearers and Geass architects who interface with C's World.
tiers.systemDesc: Back-end operators with permissions beyond conventional politics.

tiers.name: Global Command Strategists
tiers.loreDesc: Leaders who shape wars, alliances, and legitimacy narratives.
tiers.systemDesc: High-centrality nodes controlling military and information routing.

tiers.name: Battlefield Execution Elites
tiers.loreDesc: Pilots and officers who convert plans into decisive tactical outcomes.
tiers.systemDesc: Throughput units that realize strategic intent under contested conditions.

tiers.name: Mass Legitimacy Constituency
tiers.loreDesc: Citizens whose belief, fear, or refusal determines whether regimes hold.
tiers.systemDesc: Distributed acceptance layer that ultimately validates or rejects command systems.

aiInsights.casual: Code Geass looks like a mecha rebellion, but the real engine is control. Lelouch doesn't just win fights; he hacks command itself through Geass, propaganda, and political theater. Every major shift comes from changing who people obey: emperors, symbols, superweapons, or a masked revolutionary. The universe feels unstable because military power, public legitimacy, and supernatural coercion are always competing for the same steering wheel.
aiInsights.deep: The universe is a layered authority architecture with three competing stabilization models: imperial hierarchy (Britannia), coercive stasis (Schneizel's Damocles), and sacrificial legitimacy reset (Zero Requiem). Geass acts as a localized consent-bypass exploit that can override individual agency, while Knightmare and sakuradite systems determine hard-force throughput. However, durable governance is decided in the legitimacy layer: symbols, broadcasts, and perceived justice. The decisive mechanic is not raw destruction but control-graph rewiring—who can issue commands, who is trusted to enforce them, and how quickly those trust edges can be collapsed or rebuilt after anomalies like Euphemia's massacre or F.L.E.I.J.A. strikes.
